data.name: TipEditingProfile
data.value: A profile is a named set of Claude settings. '(global)' uses your shared user-level settings; named profiles let you switch between different configurations.

data.name: TipProfileSelector
data.value: Switch active Claude profile. '(global)' uses shared user settings; named profiles (e.g. 'work') are separate setting sets stored in ~/.claude/profiles/.

data.name: AutoHelpActiveProfile
data.value: Select a Claude settings profile. Choose (global) for your shared user settings, or a named profile to switch configurations.

data.name: TipManageProfiles
data.value: Open the Profiles section to create, delete, apply, or sync named profiles

data.name: ButtonOpenProject
data.comment: Alt+O — top toolbar button. The underscore is Avalonia's access-key prefix; AccessText underlines the next character when Alt is held.

data.name: TipButtonOpenProject
data.value: Open a project folder to load its .claude/settings.json files

data.name: ButtonSave
data.comment: Alt+S — top toolbar button.

data.name: TipButtonSave
data.value: Save all modified settings to disk (Ctrl+S)

data.name: ButtonExport
data.comment: Alt+E — top toolbar button.

data.name: TipButtonExport
data.value: Export the fully-merged effective config as a .zip file

data.name: AutoNameEffectiveRowsGrid
data.comment: Screen-reader name for the DataGrid that lists the merged property values per scope.

data.name: AutoHelpEffectiveRowsGrid
data.value: List of settings properties and their effective merged values across Managed, Local, Project, and User scopes. Read-only; use the editor pages to change values.
data.comment: Screen-reader help text describing the EffectiveRows DataGrid's purpose and read-only nature.

data.name: AutoNameNewPluginRef
data.comment: Screen-reader name for the text box where the user types a new plugin ref (e.g. formatter@anthropic-tools) before clicking Add.

data.name: AutoNameBrowseBackupFolder
data.comment: Disambiguates the "Browse" button next to the backup output folder TextBox.

data.name: AutoNameBrowseRestoreFolder
data.comment: Disambiguates the "Browse" button next to the restore source folder TextBox.

data.name: AutoNameBackupsGrid
data.comment: Screen-reader name for the DataGrid listing previously-created backups on the Restore tab.

data.name: TipSearchBox
data.value: Search across all settings by name or description

data.name: AutoHelpSearchBox
data.value: Type to search all settings by name or description. Click a result to navigate directly to that setting.

data.name: TipToggleTheme
data.value: Toggle between light and dark theme

data.name: ButtonReloadWindow
data.comment: Alt+R — status bar button. (F5 also reloads via the Window.KeyBindings.)

data.name: TipReloadWindow
data.value: Discard unsaved changes and reload all settings files from disk

data.name: TipCliActiveBadge
data.value: The Claude Code CLI is reading settings from this profile (set via 'Apply' in the Profiles panel or by the claudectx CLI). Open the Profiles section to apply a different profile.

data.name: TipDesktopActiveBadge
data.value: Claude Desktop is reading its config from this profile (set via 'Apply' in the Profiles panel). Open the Profiles section to apply a different Desktop profile.

data.name: TipNavigateBack
data.value: Return to the page you were on before you arrived here

data.name: AutoHelpNavigateBack
data.value: Returns to the page you were viewing before clicking a deep link

data.name: TextInstallBannerDesc
data.value: Claude Code was not found on this system. You can still browse, create, and export config files — useful for setting up other machines or sharing configurations with colleagues.

data.name: LabelInstallCommand
data.value: Install on this machine with command:

data.name: TipButtonCopyInstall
data.value: Copy the install command to clipboard

data.name: TipButtonRunInstall
data.value: Open a terminal and run the install command

data.name: TipButtonDismissInstall
data.value: Hide this message for the rest of this session

data.name: TipButtonDismissSchema
data.value: Hide this banner. It will reappear if you open a different project, click Reload Window, or restart the app.

data.name: TextInstallPostInstallNoteUnix
data.value: After installing: if Claude Code lands in ~/.local/bin and that directory isn't on your PATH, click "Add to PATH" on the Version Information page (or run the installer's printed export command in your shell). Then restart ClaudeForge to detect the installation.

data.name: TextInstallPostInstallNoteWindows
data.value: After installing: restart ClaudeForge to detect the new installation (the running app sees the old PATH).

data.name: TipLinkClaudeOverview
data.value: Open Claude overview page in your browser

data.name: TipLinkQuickStart
data.value: Open Claude Code quick start guide in your browser

data.name: WelcomeHeading
data.comment: title-bar AppTitle is now "ClaudeForge" alone; the Welcome page heading still uses a longer-form descriptor since it has the visual space.

data.name: WelcomeSubtitle
data.value: Cross-platform, scope-aware settings editor for Claude Code and Claude Desktop.
data.comment: One-liner shown under the Welcome H1; mirrors LabelAboutTagline so the About dialog and the Welcome page describe the app identically. The "how to navigate" hint that used to live here is redundant with Section 1 below, which already tells the user contextually which file Save will write.

data.name: WelcomeHint
data.value: Settings are layered: Managed > User > Project > Local

data.name: TipRefreshAll
data.value: Re-scan both profile directories and update both lists.

data.name: TipNewCliProfile
data.value: Create a new CLI profile, seeded from the current live ~/.claude/ settings.

data.name: TipApplyToCli
data.value: Copy this profile's files to ~/.claude/ so the Claude Code CLI reads them on its next run. Auto-saves the currently-active profile first.

data.name: TipSyncFromLive
data.value: Update this profile's stored files with the current live ~/.claude/settings.json, CLAUDE.md, and mcpServers.

data.name: TipDeleteCliProfile
data.value: Permanently delete this profile directory. The live ~/.claude/ files are not affected.

data.name: TipButtonExportProfile
data.value: Export this profile to a single JSON file. Uses the same format as the claudectx CLI tool, so the resulting file can be imported by either app.

data.name: TipButtonImportProfile
data.value: Import a profile from a JSON file. Accepts files exported by either ClaudeForge or the claudectx CLI tool (https://github.com/foxj77/claudectx).

data.name: StatusProfileExportedFmt
data.comment: {0} is the profile name, {1} is the destination file path.

data.name: StatusProfileImportedFmt
data.comment: {0} is the resolved profile name (after override resolution).

data.name: TextClaudectxBannerBody
data.value: The "Apply to CLI" button below switches the live ~/.claude/ files (settings.json, CLAUDE.md, mcpServers in ~/.claude.json) using pure file I/O — no external tool required. The claudectx CLI tool is an OPTIONAL terminal alternative that drives the same switch through the shared active-profile pointer at ~/.claude/.claudectx-current; install it only if you want a CLI workflow. A profile created by either tool is immediately usable by the other. Export/Import below produces and consumes the claudectx single-file JSON share format.

data.name: LinkClaudectxRepo
data.comment: Public URL of the claudectx repository — surfaces on the Profiles page banner as a clickable link.

data.name: FilePickerProfileJson
data.comment: File picker filter display name for *.json profile export/import files.

data.name: TipHeaderProfileName
data.value: Profile name — matches the subdirectory under ~/.claude/profiles/

data.name: TipHeaderCliActive
data.value: Green badge: this profile is currently applied to ~/.claude/ — the Claude Code CLI reads from this profile.

data.name: TipCliActivePill
data.value: This profile's files are currently in ~/.claude/ — the Claude Code CLI is reading from this profile.

data.name: TipHeaderClaudeMd
data.value: This profile includes a custom CLAUDE.md global instruction file

data.name: TipHeaderMcpServers
data.value: This profile includes custom MCP server definitions

data.name: TipNewDesktopProfile
data.value: Create a new Desktop profile, seeded from the current live claude_desktop_config.json.

data.name: TextDesktopNotInstalled
data.value: Claude Desktop does not appear to be installed on this machine. Desktop profiles will become available once Claude Desktop is installed.

data.name: TipApplyToDesktop
data.value: Copy this profile's claude_desktop_config.json to the live Desktop location. Claude Desktop picks up the change on its next launch. Auto-saves the currently-active Desktop profile first.

data.name: TipSyncDesktopFromLive
data.value: Update this profile with the current live claude_desktop_config.json.

data.name: TipDeleteDesktopProfile
data.value: Permanently delete this Desktop profile. The live Desktop config is not affected.

data.name: TipHeaderDesktopActive
data.value: Blue badge: this profile's config is currently applied to the live Desktop config location.

data.name: TipDesktopActivePill
data.value: This profile's config is currently applied to the live Claude Desktop config location.

data.name: TipHeaderDesktopProfileName
data.value: Profile name — matches the subdirectory under the Desktop profiles directory

data.name: WatermarkBackupDir
data.value: No folder chosen — click Browse to set one

data.name: TextBackupDirHint
data.value: Backup archives (.zip) are written to this folder. The restore tab can scan a different folder.

data.name: TextNoBackupDirWarning
data.value: ⚠ No output folder selected. Click Browse to choose where backup files will be saved.

data.name: RadioSanitizedBackup
data.value: Sanitized for sharing (secrets redacted — not restorable)

data.name: TipRadioSanitizedBackup
data.value: Same file scope as Settings-only, but every *.json value whose key matches the sensitive-keys classifier (env, headers, credentials, auth, *token, *secret, *password, *apikey, bearer) is replaced with the literal string "[redacted]". Intended for sharing your configuration shape with support, the community, or a bug report without leaking API keys, MCP auth headers, or OAuth tokens. Sanitized archives cannot be restored — choose Settings-only or Full for a recoverable backup.

data.name: TextBackupSecretsWarning
data.value: ⚠ This mode preserves secrets verbatim. API keys, OAuth tokens, and MCP authorization headers will be stored in plaintext inside the archive. Keep the .zip file private; choose Sanitized mode below if you need to share the backup.

data.name: TextSanitizedModeExplainer
data.value: Sanitized backup: secret-bearing values will be replaced with "[redacted]" so the archive is safe to share. Credentials are skipped entirely. The result CANNOT be restored — it's a sharing artefact, not a recovery point.

data.name: TipIncludeCredentials
data.value: Only check this if the backup file will stay on trusted storage. Anyone with the backup can log in as you while those tokens remain valid.

data.name: WatermarkRestoreDir
data.value: No folder chosen — click Browse to locate backups

data.name: TextNoRestoreDirWarning
data.value: ⚠ No folder selected. Click Browse to choose the folder containing your backup archives.

data.name: TextChooseRestoreFolder
data.value: Choose a folder above to see available backups.

data.name: TextRestoreInstructions
data.value: Select a backup to restore. Existing files will be moved aside as .pre-restore-*.bak before being overwritten.

data.name: TipHeaderBackupDate
data.value: Date and time this backup was created (local time)

data.name: TipHeaderBackupFile
data.value: Name of the backup archive file on disk

data.name: TipHeaderBackupPlatform
data.value: OS platform that created this backup: windows, macos, or linux. Restoring a backup from a different platform may require manual path edits.

data.name: TipHeaderBackupMode
data.value: Backup scope — SettingsOnly excludes session transcripts and project memory; Full includes everything under ~/.claude/.

data.name: TipHeaderBackupClients
data.value: Which Claude clients were included: ClaudeCode and/or ClaudeDesktop

data.name: TipHeaderBackupSize
data.value: Compressed size of the backup archive

data.name: TipHeaderBackupActions
data.value: Restore overwrites your live settings with this backup (existing files are backed up as .pre-restore-*.bak). Delete permanently removes the archive.

data.name: TipButtonShareBackup
data.value: Send this backup archive to another app or device via the OS share panel

data.name: TextMsixDesc
data.value: Claude Desktop is installed as an MSIX package. Windows virtualises the app's writes to a per-package AppData path. That causes the 'Edit Config' button to open the wrong file, and tooling (including this app) to see a stale copy. Creating an NTFS junction from the standard path to the virtualised path fixes both.

data.name: TextEnvSubtitle
data.value: Claude's own process environment. For per-server credentials (API keys etc.), use the env section inside each MCP server instead.

data.name: TipRefreshEnv
data.value: Re-read the current process environment and reload all stored values from disk

data.name: TextShowCommonVars
data.value: Showing common dev variables. Check 'Show all' to see everything.

data.name: TextMacLinuxNote
data.value: ℹ On macOS / Linux, User and Machine environment stores are not persistently writable via .NET. Only the Claude env scope is available for editing.

data.name: TipHeaderEnvName
data.value: Environment variable name. Hover for a description; click to view/edit in the detail pane.

data.name: TipHeaderEffectiveValue
data.value: The resolved value Claude reads, after all environment scopes are applied (process wins over user wins over Claude-stored)

data.name: TipHeaderSource
data.value: Which scope provides the effective value: process (OS env), claude (stored in claude env config), user (Windows user env), or machine (Windows machine env)

data.name: TipHeaderOverridden
data.value: Checked when a narrower scope (process or claude) sets this variable explicitly, taking priority over stored values

data.name: TextSelectVariable
data.value: Select a variable to view its per-scope values.

data.name: TextProcessEnvNote
data.value: ℹ This variable is set in the process environment and cannot be edited here. It will override any stored value.

data.name: StatusEnvKeyInvalidFmt
data.value: '{0}' is not a valid environment variable name.

data.name: TipButtonOpenConfig
data.value: Open the primary config file in the default text editor

data.name: ButtonManageProfiles
data.comment: Toolbar button next to the Editing-Profile dropdown; navigates to the Profiles section. Replaces the ✏ pencil glyph that rendered as a missing-glyph box on some Linux installations (CachyOS smoke). Alt+M.

data.name: ButtonRevealInExplorer
data.comment: DEPRECATED — kept for back-compat with any external consumers, but new call sites should bind {x:Static loc:PlatformLabels.Reveal} which resolves to the platform-correct label (Reveal in Explorer / Reveal in Finder / Show in Files).

data.name: TextMemoryViewerEditHintWindows
data.value: To edit, open in your text editor — click "Reveal in Explorer".

data.name: TextMemoryViewerEditHintMac
data.value: To edit, open in your text editor — click "Reveal in Finder".

data.name: TextMemoryViewerEditHintLinux
data.value: To edit, open in your text editor — click "Show in Files".

data.name: TipButtonShareLog
data.value: Share the current log file (useful for bug reports)

data.name: TipButtonShareConfig
data.value: Share the effective settings as JSON text

data.name: TextClaudeCodeNotOnPath
data.value: Found at {0}, but the containing directory is not on your PATH. Running `claude` from a terminal will fail until PATH is updated.

data.name: TipButtonAddClaudeCodeToPath
data.value: Add the directory containing claude to your user PATH (no administrator rights required). Open a new terminal for the change to take effect.

data.name: TextAddToPathAdded
data.value: Added to user PATH. Open a new terminal to use `claude`.

data.name: TextAddToPathAlreadyPresent
data.value: Directory was already on PATH (your current session may be stale).

data.name: TextAddToPathFailed
data.value: Could not update PATH — edit `HKCU\Environment\Path` manually or contact support.

data.name: TextAddToPathUnsupported
data.value: Automatic PATH editing is not supported on this platform. Add the directory to your shell rc file manually.

data.name: DialogMessageAddClaudeCodeToPath
TipButtonCopyAllChanges
data.value: Copy a plain-text summary of all changes to the clipboard (no descriptions)

data.name: TipCheckboxShowDialog
data.value: Uncheck to skip this confirmation dialog and save immediately in future

data.name: DialogTitleSaveChanges
data.comment: Window title for the SaveChangesDialog when shown in Save mode (regular save flow + pre-Backup save guard).

data.name: DialogTitleRestorePreview
data.comment: Window title for the SaveChangesDialog when shown in Restore mode (pre-Restore save guard).

data.name: LabelMcpDescription
data.comment: MCP server description field (optional human-readable blurb).

data.name: WatermarkMcpDescription
data.value: Optional — short description shown in tooltips

data.name: LabelDisableBypassPermissionsMode
data.comment: Permissions toggle that prevents `defaultMode = bypassPermissions` from taking effect; managed-scope org policy.

data.name: TextDisableBypassPermissionsModeHint
data.value: When set, Claude refuses to run in bypassPermissions mode regardless of any user-scope override. Typically configured at Managed scope by org policy.

data.name: LabelAdditionalDirectories
data.comment: Extra directories the permission scope extends into.

data.name: TextAdditionalDirectoriesHint
data.value: Paths outside the project root that Claude is allowed to consider in scope. One absolute path per row.

data.name: HeaderHookTimeout
data.comment: Per-hook timeout column header in the hooks DataGrid.

data.name: TipHookTimeout
data.value: Optional per-hook timeout in seconds. Leave blank to use the CLI default.

data.name: LabelHookHeaders
data.comment: Header row for the URL-hook headers editor.

data.name: TextHookHeadersHint
data.value: Custom HTTP headers added to the request when this URL hook fires. Use ${VARNAME} to interpolate values from environment variables listed below.

data.name: LabelHookAllowedEnvVars
data.comment: Header row for the URL-hook allowed-env-vars editor.

data.name: TextHookAllowedEnvVarsHint
data.value: Environment variable names whose values may be interpolated into header values via ${VARNAME}. Names not listed here will not be substituted.

data.name: TextMcpEnvVarsHint
data.value: Injected into this server's subprocess only — not visible to Claude or other servers. Use this for API keys and credentials this tool needs.

data.name: ValidationMcpCommandRequired
data.value: Command is required for stdio transport.

data.name: ValidationMcpUrlInvalid
data.value: URL must be a valid http:// or https:// address.

data.name: TextHookValidationWarning
data.value: One or more hooks has an empty Matcher or Command — those hooks will not fire.

data.name: TextDefaultModeHint
data.value: Controls how Claude handles tool calls not covered by any Allow, Deny, or Ask rule. Hover over each option for a description.

data.name: TextPermissionsExplainer
data.value: Permission rules control which Claude Code tools can run without prompting. A rule is either a bare tool name — such as Read or Bash — granting unrestricted access to that tool, or a parameterized form such as Bash(git *) or Read(./**/*.ts) that matches only invocations whose argument fits the pattern. Rules in Allow run silently; Deny rules are blocked outright; Ask rules always trigger a confirmation prompt regardless of the Default Mode setting.

data.name: TextCommonActionsNote
data.value: Filtered to hide rules already present in any scope.

data.name: TextCommonActionsEmpty
data.value: All common rules are already configured.

data.name: TextCommonActionsWslHint
data.value: Windows tip: Claude's Bash tool runs through Git Bash / MSYS by default, which can mangle paths on filesystem-heavy commands. Allow-list the WSL group below (e.g. Bash(wsl git status)) and Claude will prefer the WSL path automatically when those rules are present.
data.comment: Permissions page Common Actions contextual hint — shown only when PlatformInfo.Current.IsWindows is true. Explains why the WSL group is there and how to use it. Paired with TextWelcomeWslHint on the Welcome page for cross-discoverability.

data.name: TipPermissionRule
data.value: Enter a rule: bare tool name (e.g. Read) or parameterized form (e.g. Bash(git *)). See examples below.

data.name: TipCommonAddAllow
data.value: Add this rule to the Allow list — runs silently without prompting

data.name: TipCommonAddDeny
data.value: Add this rule to the Deny list — blocked outright

data.name: TipCommonAddAsk
data.value: Add this rule to the Ask list — always triggers a confirmation prompt

data.name: TipResetToInherited
data.value: Remove this setting at the current scope (inherit from lower scope)

data.name: TipUndocumented
data.value: Undocumented setting — not in official Claude documentation

data.name: TipNewSetting
data.value: New setting — added since your last session

data.name: TipHeaderEffectiveProperty
data.value: Setting name (JSON key) as used in settings.json

data.name: TipHeaderEffectiveValueCol
data.value: The merged effective value — the actual value Claude reads, after all layers are applied

data.name: TipHeaderEffectiveScope
data.value: Which config layer defines this value: local (machine-only) > project (shared in repo) > user (personal default) > managed (org policy)

data.name: TipHeaderEffectiveOverridden
data.value: Checked when a narrower scope (e.g. local or project) sets this property explicitly, overriding this row's value

data.name: StatusNothingToSave
data.value: Nothing to save — settings not loaded yet.

data.name: StatusNothingToExport
data.value: Nothing to export — settings not loaded yet.

data.name: TitleUnsavedChangesOnClose
data.comment: Window title of the modal that appears when the user closes the main window with pending edits.

data.name: AppTitle
data.comment: Main window title prefix. MainWindowViewModel.WindowTitle appends " — <branch|folder|No Project Loaded>" and a " *" suffix when there are unsaved edits.

data.name: TitleNoProjectLoaded
data.comment: Appended to the AppTitle in the window titlebar when no project root is set (user has not opened a project folder). Format: "ClaudeForge — No Project Loaded".

data.name: TextUnsavedChangesOnClose
data.value:
You have unsaved changes. What would you like to do?
Save — write your edits to disk and close. Don't Save — discard your edits and close. Cancel — keep the window open.
data.comment: Body of the unsaved-changes-on-close modal. Three explicit choices so the user can't accidentally lose work.

data.name: TipBadgeSaveDialogOff
data.value: The "Show this dialog on save" preference is unchecked this session — saves will write directly without showing a confirmation dialog. Click "Reload Window" to re-enable, or save once more and re-check the box.

data.name: LabelSchemaBannerTitleFmt
data.value: ⚠ {0} schema issue(s) found in loaded config
data.comment: {0} is the integer count of validation errors. Banner at the top of the main window when validation fails after a workspace reload.

data.name: LabelSchemaBannerDesc
data.value: One or more values on disk don't match the expected schema. The app keeps working — you can save other changes — but Claude may reject the offending fields at runtime. Click "Show details" for the field-by-field list.

data.name: LabelCommonActionKindRead
data.comment: Chiclet label for non-mutating Common Actions rules (cat, ls, git status, Read).

data.name: LabelCommonActionKindWrite
data.comment: Chiclet label for local-state-mutating Common Actions rules (edit, git add, git commit).

data.name: LabelCommonActionKindNetwork
data.comment: Chiclet label for external-I/O Common Actions rules (npm, dotnet, curl, WebFetch, mcp__*).

data.name: LabelCommonActionKindDestructive
data.comment: Chiclet label for irreversible / broad-blast-radius Common Actions rules (git push, raw Bash).

data.name: LabelCommonActionsCatchAll
data.value: All Tools — wildcard rules that apply across every tool
data.comment: Heading rendered above the catch-all wildcard tier at the bottom of the Common Actions list.

data.name: NavTitleMemory
data.comment: Top-level nav node label for the Memory page.

data.name: NavDescMemory
data.value: Audit, view, and clean up the files Claude reads on every session and the behavioural footprint left on disk.
data.comment: Top-level nav node description for the Memory page.

data.name: HeaderMemoryCode
data.comment: Section header above the Tier 1 / Tier 2 tabs on the Memory page (Code section).

data.name: HeaderMemoryDesktop
data.comment: Section header above the Desktop explainer panel on the Memory page.

data.name: HeaderTabUserMemory
data.comment: Tab header for the Tier 1 (read-only) user-memory inventory.

data.name: HeaderTabFootprint
data.comment: Tab header for the Tier 2 footprint stats + cleanup table.

data.name: LabelMemoryViewerNote
data.value: To edit, open in your text editor — click "Reveal in Explorer".
data.comment: Info note shown at the top of the read-only Tier 1 file viewer.

data.name: ButtonRefresh
data.comment: Generic Refresh button label used on Memory page tabs.

data.name: ButtonReveal
data.comment: DEPRECATED — bind {x:Static loc:PlatformLabels.Reveal} instead so macOS shows "Reveal in Finder" and Linux shows "Show in Files".

data.name: ButtonCloseViewer
data.comment: Button that closes the Tier 1 file viewer and returns to the inventory.

data.name: ButtonCopyMarkdown
data.comment: Button on the Tier 1 file viewer toolbar that copies the raw markdown source to the clipboard.

data.name: ButtonOpenInEditor
data.comment: Button that opens a file in the platform's registered default editor for the file's type. Used on the Memory page Tier 1 file viewer toolbar, Tier 1 inventory rows, Tier 2 footprint rows, and per-project transcript rows.

data.name: LabelFileCountFmt
data.comment: Inline summary on a Tier 1 group expander header. {0} = file count.

data.name: HeaderInStandardBackup
data.comment: Tier 2 footprint table column header.

data.name: TitleDeleteFootprintFmt
data.comment: Destructive dialog title. {0} = footprint category human label.

data.name: MsgDeleteFootprintConfirmFmt
data.value: This will permanently delete {0} file(s) totalling {1} from disk:
data.comment: Destructive dialog body. {0} = file count, {1} = humanised size.

data.name: ButtonDeleteFootprint
data.comment: Confirm-button label on the Destructive footprint-delete dialog.

data.name: LabelDesktopMemoryExplainerTitle
data.value: Claude Desktop has no equivalent user-memory file.

data.name: LabelDesktopMemoryExplainerBody
data.value: Claude Desktop reads its configuration from a single config.json and stores conversation history inside an opaque IndexedDB store managed by Chromium. The Memory page surfaces user-authored memory only for Claude Code; for Desktop, use the app's own "Clear chat history" command for privacy actions.

data.name: TipMemoryCategoryPrimary
data.value: Top-level CLAUDE.md (or AGENTS.md) Claude reads at the start of every Code session — your project-agnostic preferences and standing instructions.

data.name: TipMemoryCategoryProject
data.value: CLAUDE.md (or AGENTS.md) at the open project's root — preferences scoped to the current project, layered on top of the primary memory.

data.name: TipMemoryCategorySubagents
data.value: Custom subagent definitions in ~/.claude/agents/. Each .md file describes a named agent with its own system prompt, tools, and triggering conditions.

data.name: TipMemoryCategoryCommands
data.value: Custom slash commands in ~/.claude/commands/. Each .md file maps a /name invocation to a parameterised prompt.

data.name: TipMemoryCategoryHooks
data.value: Scripts run by the hook system at session lifecycle events (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, etc.). Stored in ~/.claude/hooks/ — any executable file extension is accepted.

data.name: TipMemoryCategoryPlans
data.value: Saved plans in ~/.claude/plans/. Each .md captures a structured implementation plan that survives across sessions.

data.name: TipMemoryCategoryRules
data.value: Rule files in ~/.claude/rules/ (recursive). Markdown documents describing coding conventions, security policies, and personal standards Claude follows.

data.name: TipMemoryCategorySkills
data.value: Custom skills in ~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md. Each skill bundles task-specific knowledge Claude can invoke when its description matches the user's intent.

data.name: TipMemoryCategoryCrossTool
data.value: Memory files for sibling AI tools (.codex/AGENTS.md, .gemini/GEMINI.md, .opencode/*.md) that Claude reads as cross-tool context.

data.name: TipFootprintCategoryTranscripts
data.value: Every session's full conversation transcript stored as JSONL under ~/.claude/projects/<mangled>/. Largest single contributor to disk usage; not preserved in Standard backups.

data.name: TipFootprintCategorySessions
data.value: Per-session metadata: ~/.claude/sessions/, session-data/, session-env/. Session-scoped state, environment captures, and resume hints.

data.name: TipFootprintCategoryHistory
data.value: ~/.claude/history.jsonl — every interactive prompt you've submitted, persisted across sessions so the Up-arrow recalls past inputs.

data.name: TipFootprintCategoryBashLog
data.value: ~/.claude/bash-commands.log — log of bash invocations Claude has run on your behalf, useful for auditing what shell activity has occurred.

data.name: TipFootprintCategoryCostLog
data.value: ~/.claude/cost-tracker.log — token-cost telemetry per session for cost analysis.

data.name: TipFootprintCategoryTodos
data.value: ~/.claude/todos/ — todo-list snapshots from the TodoWrite tool, scoped per session.

data.name: TipFootprintCategoryFileHistory
data.value: ~/.claude/file-history/ — before/after snapshots of files Claude has edited, used by the Edit tool's diff display.

data.name: HeaderProjectTranscriptBreakdown
data.comment: Expander header for the per-project transcript drilldown on the Footprint tab.

data.name: TipProjectTranscriptBreakdown
data.value: One row per project under ~/.claude/projects/. Lets you wipe a single project's transcripts without touching the others — finer-grained than the Session transcripts category-level delete above.
data.comment: Tooltip on the per-project breakdown Expander header.

data.name: TitleDeleteProjectTranscriptsFmt
data.comment: Destructive dialog title when deleting a single project's transcripts. {0} = decoded project display name.

data.name: MsgDeleteProjectTranscriptsConfirmFmt
data.value: This will permanently delete {0} transcript file(s) totalling {1} from this project's directory:
data.comment: Destructive dialog body for per-project transcript delete. {0} = file count, {1} = humanised size.

data.name: HeaderProjectColumnLastSeen
data.comment: Column header for the relative-time column in the per-project breakdown.

data.name: HeaderFootprintLargeWarning
data.comment: Banner header on the Footprint tab when total disk usage exceeds the warning threshold.

data.name: MsgFootprintLargeWarningFmt
data.value: Your Claude footprint is currently {0}. Consider expanding the Per-project breakdown below to wipe transcripts from projects you no longer work on.
data.comment: Banner body. {0} = humanised aggregate size (e.g. "12.4 GB").

data.name: HeaderAdvancedFields
data.comment: Per-row Expander header surfacing optional ref / path sub-fields on github / git Marketplace variants.

data.name: LabelMarketplaceRef
data.comment: Label for the optional git ref (branch / tag / SHA) sub-field on a Marketplace row.

data.name: WatermarkMarketplaceRef
data.comment: Placeholder text for the ref TextBox.

data.name: LabelMarketplacePath
data.comment: Label for the optional subdirectory path sub-field on a Marketplace row.

data.name: WatermarkMarketplacePath
data.comment: Placeholder text for the path TextBox.

data.name: HeaderHttpHeaders
data.comment: Per-row Expander header surfacing the HTTP headers sub-editor on a Marketplace url variant.

data.name: WatermarkHeaderName
data.comment: Placeholder for the new-header name TextBox (e.g. Authorization).

data.name: WatermarkHeaderValue
data.comment: Placeholder for the new-header value TextBox (e.g. Bearer xyz).

data.name: WatermarkPluginId
data.comment: Placeholder for the per-row plugin id TextBox (format: plugin-name@marketplace-id).

data.name: WatermarkPluginMcpServerName
data.comment: Placeholder for the per-server name TextBox inside a plugin's mcpServers map.

data.name: WatermarkPluginConfigKey
data.comment: Placeholder for the per-config key TextBox inside an MCP server config map.

data.name: WatermarkPluginConfigValue
data.comment: Placeholder for the per-config value TextBox.

data.name: ButtonAddPlugin
data.comment: Add-button label for adding a new plugin entry to pluginConfigs.

data.name: ButtonAddServer
data.comment: Add-button label for adding a new MCP server entry inside a plugin.

data.name: TipCommonActionKindRead
data.value: Read — non-mutating: list, search, view, status. The tool can inspect content but not change anything on disk or call out to the network.
data.comment: Tooltip on the green Read chiclet shown on every Common Actions row.

data.name: TipCommonActionKindWrite
data.value: Write — modifies local state: edit, save, add, commit. Changes stay on this machine and can be undone with version control or backups.
data.comment: Tooltip on the amber Write chiclet shown on every Common Actions row.

data.name: TipCommonActionKindNetwork
data.value: Network — calls out to remote services: package installs, web fetches, MCP servers. Granting this lets the tool reach the internet.
data.comment: Tooltip on the blue Network chiclet shown on every Common Actions row.

data.name: TipCommonActionKindDestructive
data.value: Destructive — irreversible or broad blast radius: git push, raw shell wildcards. Once granted, recovery may require external action.
data.comment: Tooltip on the red Destructive chiclet shown on every Common Actions row.

data.name: LabelEditingScope
data.comment: Caption above the scope combo box on every settings page.

data.name: TipEditingScopeLabel
data.value: The scope (layer) determines which config file this setting is written to. Lower scopes override higher ones: local > project > user > managed.
data.comment: Tooltip on the "Editing scope:" label.

data.name: TipEditingScopeCombo
data.value: Choose the config layer to write to. 'local' affects only your current machine; 'project' is shared with the repo; 'user' is your personal default; 'managed' is org-controlled and read-only.
data.comment: Tooltip on the scope ComboBox.

data.name: AutoNameEditingScope
data.comment: AutomationProperties.Name on the scope ComboBox.

data.name: AutoHelpEditingScope
data.value: Select the configuration layer to write changes to. Narrower scopes take precedence over wider ones.
data.comment: AutomationProperties.HelpText on the scope ComboBox.

data.name: TipEditorErrorCopy
data.value: Click and Ctrl+A then Ctrl+C to copy this error
data.comment: Tooltip on the inline editor-error SelectableTextBlock that appears next to the scope combo when a SetValue throws.

data.name: HeaderTabProperties
data.comment: Tab header on the SettingsGroupEditor view.

data.name: LabelClearPropertyFilter
data.comment: Button text for the clear-filter button on the property-filter bar.

data.name: TipClearPropertyFilter
data.value: Clear the property filter and show all properties
data.comment: Tooltip on the clear-property-filter button.

data.name: AutoNameClearPropertyFilter
data.comment: AutomationProperties.Name on the clear-property-filter button.

data.name: HeaderTabEffective
data.comment: Tab header on the SettingsGroupEditor view (Effective Values tab).

data.name: LabelCopyToClipboard
data.comment: Generic copy-to-clipboard button label, reused across editor JSON views.

data.name: TipCopyJsonClipboard
data.value: Copy the JSON shown below to the clipboard
data.comment: Tooltip on the copy-JSON-to-clipboard button on the JSON tab of SettingsGroupEditor.

data.name: AutoNameCopyJsonClipboard
data.comment: AutomationProperties.Name on the copy-JSON-to-clipboard button.

data.name: LabelShowAllSchemaProps
data.value: Show all schema properties (including unset defaults)
data.comment: Checkbox label on the JSON tab of SettingsGroupEditor that toggles whether unset defaults are rendered.

data.name: TipShowAllSchemaProps
data.value: Checked: shows every schema-defined property with its default value or a placeholder, useful as a reference. Unchecked: shows only properties that have a value set in the active scope.
data.comment: Tooltip on the "Show all schema properties" checkbox.

data.name: AutoNameShowAllSchemaProps
data.comment: AutomationProperties.Name on the "Show all schema properties" checkbox.

data.name: AutoHelpShowAllSchemaProps
data.value: When checked, the JSON view includes every schema property with default or placeholder values. When unchecked, only set values are shown.
data.comment: AutomationProperties.HelpText on the "Show all schema properties" checkbox.

data.name: HeaderSaveDialogProperty
data.comment: Save-confirmation dialog: "Property" column header.

data.name: HeaderSaveDialogOld
data.comment: Save-confirmation dialog: "Old value" column header.

data.name: HeaderSaveDialogNew
data.comment: Save-confirmation dialog: "New value" column header.

data.name: SaveDialogKindAdded
data.comment: Tooltip + screen-reader name for the green '+' kind pill in the Save-changes dialog.

data.name: SaveDialogKindRemoved
data.comment: Tooltip + screen-reader name for the red '-' kind pill in the Save-changes dialog.

data.name: SaveDialogKindModified
data.comment: Tooltip + screen-reader name for the orange '~' kind pill in the Save-changes dialog.

data.name: LabelMenuCopy
data.comment: Generic "Copy" context-menu item label, used in SaveChangesDialog cell context-flyouts and EffectiveSettings cells.

data.name: WatermarkMarketplaceName
data.comment: Marketplaces editor: placeholder text for the marketplace-name field (both new-row and existing-row inline).

data.name: WatermarkMarketplaceSource
data.comment: Marketplaces editor: placeholder text for the marketplace source/url field.

data.name: LabelMarketplaceAdd
data.comment: Marketplaces editor: button text for adding a new marketplace.

data.name: WatermarkMarketplaceNameInline
data.comment: Marketplaces editor: shorter placeholder text for the inline name field on existing rows.

data.name: LabelOperationReadOnly
data.comment: Common Actions operation-group header for non-mutating File-tool rules.

data.name: LabelOperationWrite
data.comment: Common Actions operation-group header for File-tool write rules.

data.name: LabelOperationSearchView
data.comment: Common Actions operation-group header for shell-tool view/search commands.

data.name: LabelOperationGitRead
data.comment: Common Actions operation-group header for git status/log/diff.

data.name: LabelOperationGitWrite
data.comment: Common Actions operation-group header for git add/commit/push.

data.name: LabelOperationRuntimes
data.comment: Common Actions operation-group header for interpreter/runtime invocations (dotnet/npm/python/node).

data.name: LabelOperationNetwork
data.comment: Common Actions operation-group header for outbound network commands (curl, wget, Invoke-WebRequest).

data.name: LabelOperationFetchSearch
data.comment: Common Actions operation-group header for the Web tool's WebFetch/WebSearch operations.

data.name: LabelOperationWildcards
data.comment: Common Actions operation-group header inside the catch-all wildcard tier.

data.name: TextNoTerminalFound
data.value: No terminal found — use Copy instead

data.name: TipButtonOpenDownload
data.value: Open the Claude Desktop download page in your default browser

data.name: TipButtonCopyUrl
data.value: Copy the download URL to clipboard

data.name: DialogPromptNewProfile
data.value: Enter a name for the new profile:

data.name: MsgReservedProfileName
data.value: '{0}' is a reserved name. Please choose a different profile name.

data.name: MsgInvalidPathChars
data.value: Profile name contains characters that are not allowed in file names.

data.name: MsgProfileAlreadyExists
data.value: A profile named '{0}' already exists. Choose a different name.

data.name: MsgConfirmDeleteProfile
data.value: Delete profile '{0}' and all its settings? This cannot be undone.

data.name: StatusProfileCreatedFmt
data.value: Profile '{0}' created from current live settings.

data.name: StatusChooseBackupFolder
data.value: Choose an output folder first (click Browse next to the folder path).

data.name: StatusClaudeRunningFmt
data.value: [!] Claude is currently running ({0} process(es)). Some files may be locked during restore.

data.name: StatusNoCliProfiles
data.value: No CLI profiles found. Create one with the New button.

data.name: StatusProfileAppliedFmt
data.value: Profile '{0}' applied. Claude Code will use these settings on its next run.

data.name: StatusNoDesktopProfiles
data.value: No Desktop profiles found. Create one with the New button.

data.name: StatusDesktopProfileCreatedFmt
data.value: Desktop profile '{0}' created from current live config.

data.name: StatusSyncingDesktopProfileFmt
data.value: Syncing live Desktop config into profile '{0}'…

data.name: StatusDesktopProfileSyncedFmt
data.value: Desktop profile '{0}' updated from live config.

data.name: StatusCannotSaveClaude
data.value: Cannot save to Claude scope — no Claude Code installation was detected.

data.name: ScopeNoteClaude
data.value: Writes to the Claude Code env dict (saved with the main Save button).

data.name: ScopeNoteUser
data.value: Writes to the Windows user environment registry immediately.

data.name: ScopeNoteMachine
data.value: Writes to the Windows machine environment registry immediately (requires elevated privileges).

data.name: TextOpenProjectHint
data.value: Open a project folder to edit Project and Local scopes.

data.name: TextScopeMeaningLocal
data.value: Your machine-only overrides for this project. Highest user priority.

data.name: TextScopeMeaningProject
data.value: Shared with the team via git. Overrides User.

data.name: TextScopeMeaningUser
data.value: Your personal default for every project.

data.name: TextBackupContentsLine1
data.value: All of ~/.claude/ — settings, profiles, hooks, MCP servers.

data.name: TextBackupContentsProjectNote
data.value: Project files (.claude/ in your repo) are included only when a project is open at backup time, or added explicitly.

data.name: TextWelcomeAutoReload
data.value: Auto-reload — when another tool or editor changes a config file on disk (e.g. claudectx switching profiles, or a hand-edit in your text editor), this app picks it up automatically and refreshes the editor.

data.name: TextWelcomeEssentials
data.value: ★ Essentials (top of the nav tree) gathers the high-impact knobs — token budgets, sandbox, MCP trust, model selection — for quick review on a fresh install.

data.name: TextWelcomeMemory
data.value: 🧠 Memory surfaces what Claude knows about you (session transcripts, prompt history, todos, cost tracker, file-edit logs) and lets you erase any of it — useful for privacy hygiene before sharing your machine, recording a demo, or taking screenshots.

data.name: TextWelcomeWslHint
data.value: 🪟 Windows tip — if Claude gets tripped up by Git Bash / MSYS path mangling (backslashes, forward-slash globs, etc.) on filesystem-heavy operations, Permissions → Common Actions → WSL provides one-click allow-list patterns for the WSL-wrapped variants (e.g. Bash(wsl git status)). Claude will prefer the WSL path once those rules are present.
data.comment: Welcome page "Things to know" bullet — shown only when PlatformInfo.Current.IsWindows is true. Points Windows users at the WSL Common Actions group added as a solution to MSYS path-mangling friction.

data.name: TextSaveSilentSaveSafetyNet
data.value: You have unsaved changes, but the per-property change preview couldn't be built. Save anyway?
data.comment: Fallback dialog body shown by the silent-save safety net (SaveCoreAsync) when SDK reports HasUnsavedChanges=true but SaveDialogBuilder.Build returns null. The user-visible wording is intentionally vague — internal diagnostic context lives in the rolling log, not in the dialog.

data.name: TextEffectiveSettingsSubtitle
data.value: The fully-merged values that Claude actually sees at runtime, after layering Managed > Local > Project > User. Each row shows the winning value, the scope it came from, and whether a higher-priority scope is overriding a lower one.

data.name: TextEffectiveTabSubtitle
data.value: The actual runtime values Claude reads for the settings on this page, after layering. Properties with no value at any scope are hidden.

data.name: ButtonClearAppData
data.comment: Alt+D — status bar button. "D" rather than "C" to avoid conflict with common Cancel/Close mnemonics in dialogs.

data.name: TipClearAppData
data.value: Delete saved window position, dimensions, theme, last-selected page, and other persisted UI state. Does NOT touch your Claude config files. ClaudeForge will exit.

data.name: TextClearAppDataConfirm
data.value: This will delete the persisted UI state file at ~/.claude/cache/ClaudeForge-gui-state.json (window position, dimensions, theme, last-selected page, profile selection, backup preferences). Your Claude config files (~/.claude/settings.json and friends) are NOT affected. ClaudeForge will exit. Continue?

data.name: TextProfilesSubtitle
data.value: Named settings profiles for Claude Code and Claude Desktop. Switch between configurations, sync across products, or delete old ones.

data.name: TextBackupRestoreSubtitle
data.value: Create timestamped backup archives of your Claude config and restore from them later. Optional projects and credentials inclusion.

data.name: TipEffectiveOverriddenTrue
data.value: This property is also defined in a lower-priority scope but a higher-priority scope is winning.

data.name: TipEffectiveOverriddenFalse
data.value: This property is defined in only one scope.

data.name: TextBypassPermissionsWarning
data.value: Setting defaultMode to bypassPermissions is equivalent to launching Claude Code with the --dangerouslySkipPermissions flag. All tool permission prompts are suppressed — Claude can read, write, and execute without asking. Only use this in fully isolated or automated environments where you control all inputs.

data.name: TextNoBackupYetDescription
data.value: Use 'Back up now' to create your first snapshot. Backups are zip files that can be restored from the Restore tab.

data.name: TextRestoreDescription
data.value: Select a backup folder and choose a zip file to restore. This will overwrite your current configuration files.

data.name: TextHookMatcherGuidance
data.value: Matcher is a tool-name pattern (e.g. Bash, Edit, * matches all tools). Each hook runs when a matching tool call fires the selected event.

data.name: TipHookMatcherHeader
data.value: Tool name pattern — use * to match all tools

data.name: TipHookTypeHeader
data.value: Hook type: command runs a shell command, prompt injects a system prompt, url posts to a webhook

data.name: TipHookCommandHeader
data.value: The shell command, prompt text, or URL to invoke

data.name: TipVersionLabel
data.value: Click to open the About ClaudeForge dialog.

data.name: LabelAboutTagline
data.value: Cross-platform, scope-aware settings editor for Claude Code and Claude Desktop.

data.name: ValidationEnvVarName
data.value: Name must start with a letter or _ and contain only letters, digits, and _.

data.name: DialogTitleUnsavedChanges
data.comment: Dialog title shown when the user starts Backup or Restore with dirty workspace edits.

data.name: DialogTitleRestoreCompletedSkippedFmt
data.comment: {0} is the count of files that could not be written (locked or inaccessible).

data.name: DialogTitleDropRestore
data.comment: Title of the confirm prompt shown after a backup .zip is dropped onto the Restore tab.

data.name: DialogTitleDropRestoreInvalid
data.comment: Error-alert title when the dropped file is not a valid backup archive.

data.name: TextDropRestorePromptPrefix
data.comment: Body text: leading half of the drop-restore confirm prompt. Followed by the filename, then TextDropRestorePromptSuffix. Trailing space intentional.

data.name: TextDropRestorePromptSuffix
data.value:
?
This will overwrite your current settings with the contents of the dropped backup archive.
data.comment: Body text: trailing half of the drop-restore confirm prompt. Follows the filename.

data.name: TextDropRestoreNotZip
data.value: Only backup .zip files can be dropped here to restore.
data.comment: Error-alert body when the dropped file is not a .zip.

data.name: TextDropRestoreNotABackup
data.value: The dropped file is not a valid ClaudeForge backup archive. It may be corrupt, from a newer version, or a different kind of zip.
data.comment: Error-alert body when the dropped zip has no valid manifest.json of Kind=backup.

data.name: ButtonIncludeCredentialsConfirm
data.comment: Confirm-button label on the credentials prompt. Distinct from the checkbox label CheckboxIncludeCredentials.

data.name: TextSaveBeforeBackupPrompt
data.value: You have unsaved edits in the active workspace. Save them before backing up? Otherwise the backup will only contain what is currently on disk — your unsaved edits will not be included.

data.name: TextProceedWithoutSavingBackup
data.value: Continue with the backup? Your unsaved edits will not be in this backup, but they will remain in memory and you can save them later.

data.name: TextSaveBeforeRestorePrompt
data.value: You have unsaved edits in the active workspace. Save them before restoring?

data.name: TextDiscardUnsavedForRestore
data.value: Restoring will overwrite your current settings. Discard your unsaved edits and continue with the restore?

data.name: TextCredentialsExplainer
data.value:
contains OAuth tokens. Anyone with this backup file can log in as you while those tokens remain valid.
Only include credentials if the backup will stay on trusted storage.
Include credentials in this backup?
data.comment: Trailing text after the credentials path in the credentials confirmation prompt. Leading space is intentional — it follows a path segment.

data.name: TextRestoreSkippedExplainer
data.value: The restore finished but the following file(s) could not be written (they may be locked by a running Claude process). Close Claude and restore again if needed:
data.comment: Trailing blank line is intentional — the file list is appended to this text.

data.name: TextRestoreSkippedTrailerFmt
data.value: … and {0} more (see the log for the full list)
data.comment: {0} is the count of files beyond the first 10 shown. Leading newline is intentional.

data.name: TextCrossPlatformRestoreSuffix
data.value:
'.
Some paths inside config files may need manual edits afterward. Continue?

data.name: NavTitleWelcome
data.comment: Title of the synthetic top-of-tree Welcome nav node — selected by default on a fresh install so the orientation content is visible until the user clicks elsewhere.

data.name: NavDescWelcome
data.value: Orientation for the editor — what gets edited, how scopes layer, and where to find the high-impact settings. Shown by default on first launch.
data.comment: Tooltip / description for the Welcome nav node.

data.name: LabelShowWelcomeOnLaunch
data.comment: Checkbox label on the Welcome page that controls whether the Welcome nav node appears in the navigation tree. Unchecking removes the node; the Welcome page remains reachable by clicking the Claude Code or Claude Desktop header nodes.

data.name: TipShowWelcomeOnLaunch
data.value: When unchecked, the Welcome entry disappears from the navigation tree and won't return on the next launch. You can still reach this page by clicking the "Claude Code" or "Claude Desktop" headings in the tree. Clearing app data resets this preference.
data.comment: Tooltip / accessibility description for the Show-on-launch checkbox on the Welcome page.

data.name: NavTitleEssentials
data.comment: Title of the synthetic top-of-tree nav node that pins the high-importance settings.

data.name: NavDescEssentials
data.value: High-importance Claude Code settings — token budgets, sandbox, MCP trust, model and update channel — pinned for quick review and change.
data.comment: Tooltip / description for the Essentials nav node.

data.name: HeaderEssentials
data.comment: Heading rendered at the top of the Essentials editor view.

data.name: HeaderEssentialsBody
data.value: These settings have an outsized impact on cost, quality, security, or behaviour. Review them when you set up a new project or upgrade Claude Code.
data.comment: Sub-header explainer for the Essentials editor view.

data.name: LabelEssentialsViewInGroupFmt
data.comment: "View in <group>" deep-link button label. {0} is the home-group title (e.g. "Permissions", "Environment").

data.name: LabelEssentialsAddListEntry
data.comment: Label for the Add button on StringList cards (e.g. sandbox.allowedDomains).

data.name: LabelEssentialsRemoveListEntry
data.comment: Label for the per-row Remove button on StringList cards.

data.name: LabelEssentialsAmberCallout
data.value: You arrived here from search — this is the setting that matches your query. Edit the value below.
data.comment: One-time amber callout shown when a card is opened via a synthetic search hit.

data.name: LabelEssentialsEnvSourceHeader
data.comment: Header for the env-var "effective source" sub-row showing which of (settings.json env, OS user, OS machine) is contributing.

data.name: LabelEssentialsEnvSourceSettings
data.comment: Source label: the value comes from the env map in settings.json.

data.name: LabelEssentialsEnvSourceUser
data.comment: Source label: the value comes from the user-scope OS environment variable.

data.name: LabelEssentialsEnvSourceMachine
data.comment: Source label: the value comes from the machine-scope OS environment variable.

data.name: LabelEssentialsEnvSourceNone
data.comment: Source label: the variable is not set in any scope.

data.name: EssentialsCardMaxThinkingTokensBody
data.value: Caps how many tokens Claude can spend on extended thinking per turn (env: MAX_THINKING_TOKENS). The default may truncate complex reasoning; raise to 32000 or higher for hard architectural work.

data.name: EssentialsCardMaxOutputTokensBody
data.value: Caps the response length Claude can emit (env: CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS). The default may truncate long file edits or generated code; raise it when you see "[output truncated]".

data.name: EssentialsCardEnableAllMcpBody
data.value: When enabled, Claude trusts every MCP server defined in a project's .mcp.json without prompting. Convenient — but a hostile project repository can ship a malicious MCP server. Leave disabled and approve servers individually.

data.name: EssentialsCardEnableAllMcpDanger
data.value: Danger: every MCP server in every project you open will run with your tools and credentials. Only enable this in fully isolated environments.

data.name: EssentialsCardSandboxEnabledBody
data.value: When enabled, the bash sandbox restricts file-system writes and network egress to an allow-list. Disabling the sandbox lets every shell command Claude runs touch arbitrary files and reach arbitrary hosts.

data.name: EssentialsCardSandboxEnabledDanger
data.value: Danger: with the sandbox disabled, any tool call that runs a shell command has unrestricted file-system and network access.

data.name: EssentialsCardSandboxDomainsBody
data.value: Outbound hosts the sandbox is permitted to reach (e.g. github.com, registry.npmjs.org). Without entries here, network operations inside the sandbox fail.

data.name: EssentialsCardSandboxDomainsListPlaceholder
data.comment: Placeholder text for the Add-entry textbox on the sandbox.allowedDomains card.

data.name: EssentialsCardModelBody
data.value: Default model for Claude Code sessions. Pick a smaller model (haiku) to cut cost on routine work; pick the largest (opus) for hard architectural reasoning.

data.name: EssentialsCardEffortLevelBody
data.value: How much extended-thinking budget Claude is willing to spend per turn. Higher levels can produce better answers on hard problems but cost more.

data.name: EssentialsCardFastModeBody
data.value: Skips some optional reasoning passes for snappier responses. Trade-off: slightly lower quality on complex tasks in exchange for lower latency and cost.

data.name: EssentialsCardAutoUpdatesChannelBody
data.value: Which Claude Code release stream the binary auto-updates from (stable, latest). "latest" gets new features sooner but ships occasional regressions.

data.name: EssentialsCardAutoMemoryEnabledBody
data.value: When enabled, Claude automatically captures session-level "memory" facts (project conventions, environment, your preferences). Disable for sessions where you don't want persistent context.

data.name: EssentialsCardDisableBypassBody
data.value: Locks the permissions.defaultMode setting away from "bypassPermissions" — the mode that suppresses every tool-permission prompt. Equivalent of pinning safety on; recommended for shared / managed environments.
